scope: Local-only RealVisXL three-sample quality matrix for close-up skin/eyes, visible hands/fabric, and low-light environment coherence.

samples.focus: close-up skin, eyes, mouth, and hairline
qa_notes: Strong close-up facial realism with readable iris detail, stable eyelids, and believable skin pores.
Mouth and jawline are coherent; no obvious teeth, lip, or facial deformation defects.
Hairline and background are stable, with no blocking crop or artifact issue.

samples.focus: hands, wrist anatomy, fabric texture, and contact realism
qa_notes: Face, wardrobe, fabric weave, lighting, and seated pose are coherent.
Hands are usable for local matrix evidence but not clean final certification material; clasped contact points create mild finger/contact ambiguity.
No severe extra-finger, collision, or body-proportion failure observed, but this sample should drive future hand/contact prompt improvement.

defects.message: Clasped hands have mild finger/contact ambiguity; acceptable for local pass-with-notes but not enough for final hands certification.

samples.focus: lighting, reflections, background coherence, and low-light realism
qa_notes: Lamp/window lighting is coherent and atmospheric without crushing the subject.
Background, curtains, fabric texture, jewelry, and hair remain plausible.
Face and gaze are stable; no obvious anatomical, crop, or object-collision defect.

qa_driven_next_improvement.reason: The only material note across the matrix is mild clasped-hand contact ambiguity.
qa_driven_next_improvement.recommended_follow_up: Add a future RealVisXL hand/contact follow-up profile with clearer separated hand pose, stronger wrist/finger anatomy terms, and negative terms for fused fingers before claiming hands certification.

next_action: Keep RealVisXL base lane at runtime-smoke-complete plus local multisample pass-with-notes. Do not certify full image quality until hand/contact follow-up and target-runtime certification gates are complete.
